text.title: Figma Comment Triage Protocol Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Figma Comment Triage Protocol must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Handoff includes: current state snapshot, in-flight blockers, named successor, expected re-engagement trigger.
rule.rationale: incomplete handoffs are the single largest cross-timezone tax.

rule.statement: The receiving party acknowledges in writing within the declared SLA. Silent acknowledgement is rejected.

rationale.source: Cross-team handoff practice from the Spotify 'tribes' model retros

rule.statement: Every handoff is logged with timestamp and decision authority. Re-handoffs within N hours trigger an escalation.

example.title: Anchored application
example.code: Trigger: Daily Figma sync + comment triage (30min) Artefact: structured output meeting the contract in content/02-output-contract.xml Evidence: ≥1 named source per rule (URL, ticket, transcript_id) Decision: downstream consumer can act without re-deriving reasoning
example.note: Demonstrates the closed loop the methodology creates — trigger → artefact → decision — with auditability at each step.

example.title: Un-anchored application
example.code: Trigger: 'we should do this thing' Artefact: prose paragraph, no schema Evidence: 'industry best practice' Decision: ambiguous — every reader interprets differently
example.note: Each ambiguity above is a failure mode catalogued in content/03-failure-modes.xml. The methodology exists to prevent exactly this shape.
